name: e2e-skills
description: Generate, review, and debug Playwright and Cypress E2E tests. Four agent skills: generate tests from scratch with live browser exploration and explicit user approval; review existing specs against 19 anti-patterns grouped by P0/P1/P2 severity — P0 must-fix (name-assertion mismatch, missing Then, error swallowing, Cypress uncaught:exception suppression, always-passing assertions, bypass patterns, focused test leak, missing assertions, missing auth setup, missing await on expect, missing await on action), P1 should-fix (raw DOM queries, hard-coded sleeps, flaky test patterns, inconsistent POM usage, hardcoded credentials, direct page action API, expect.soft overuse), P2 nice-to-fix (YAGNI + zombie specs); debug failed Playwright tests from playwright-report/; debug failed Cypress tests from cypress/reports/.

interface.shortDescription: Generate, review, and debug Playwright and Cypress E2E tests.
interface.longDescription: Four agent skills shared between Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode from ./skills/: playwright-test-generator (live-browser exploration → approval gate → reviewed Playwright tests), e2e-reviewer (static review of Playwright and Cypress specs against 19 anti-patterns grouped P0/P1/P2 by severity — silent always-pass, poor diagnostics, maintenance), playwright-debugger (root-cause diagnosis from playwright-report/), cypress-debugger (root-cause diagnosis from cypress/reports/ mochawesome or JUnit). Includes a standalone scanner (scripts/e2e-smell-scan.sh) for CI use without an agent runtime.
defaultPrompt: Use playwright-test-generator when generating new Playwright E2E tests from scratch — from coverage gap analysis to live-browser exploration, approval gate, and reviewed tests.
Use e2e-reviewer when auditing existing Playwright or Cypress specs against the 19 P0/P1/P2 anti-patterns; static analysis only, not for runtime failures.
Use playwright-debugger when Playwright tests have actually failed and you need to classify root causes from a playwright-report/ directory.
Use cypress-debugger when Cypress tests have actually failed and you need to classify root causes from cypress/reports/ mochawesome or JUnit output.
